steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage makes general, timeless statements about programming languages using consistent present tense: they 'have a clear difference,' one 'is object-oriented,' and both 'require' technical knowledge. The blank verb must match this present-tense pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "struggle": present tense, consistent with the passage's timeless generalizations. ✓
  • "struggled": shifts to past tense, contradicting the general present-tense context. ✗
  • "had struggled": same problem — past tense breaks the pattern. ✗
  • "were struggling": also past tense, inconsistent with the surrounding verbs. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
